ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general bento class; no specific fish-and-teriyaki-chicken bento subclass exists. Mapped to closest available bento food product node.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated fish-and-teriyaki-chicken bento code; A0CKP (bento box meal) is the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this specific composite bento dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard fish and teriyaki chicken bento box weighs approximately 400 g and typically contains a portion of steamed white rice, a grilled fish fillet, teriyaki-glazed chicken pieces, and assorted vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Fish and Teriyaki Chicken Bento — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Fish and Teriyaki Chicken Bento (cr_id F05100052). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite bento dish. GI of 72 is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate component: white jasmine/medium-grain rice, which has a well-established GI of approximately 72 per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × net carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (72 × 92.76) / 100 ≈ 66.8. Net carbohydrate per 400g serving is (24.2 g/100g * 4) - (1.01 g/100g * 4) = 96.8 - 4.04 = 92.76g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 217 mg/100 g; a 400 g bento portion delivers approximately 868 mg sodium, representing 37% of the 2,300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sodium teriyaki sauce or less sauce, as the soy-sauce-based glaze is the primary sodium contributor; limiting portion size to 250–300 g can reduce sodium intake to a more manageable 540–650 mg per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 24.2 g/100 g predominantly from white rice (GI ~72); a 400 g bento delivers approximately 97 g carbohydrate with only 4 g dietary fibre, producing a high glycaemic load.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should substitute white rice with brown rice or mixed-grain rice to lower the GI, reduce the rice portion to no more than 150 g cooked, and request less teriyaki sauce to minimise added sugars and glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat 7.33 g/100 g with estimated saturated fat 1.8 g/100 g; fish component provides beneficial omega-3 fatty acids, but sodium load from teriyaki sauce is a cardiovascular risk factor.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular health, the fish component is beneficial due to omega-3 content; however, sodium should be minimised by requesting reduced-sodium sauce, and portion size should be controlled to keep total saturated fat and sodium within daily cardiovascular targets.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Fish provides anti-inflammatory omega-3 fatty acids; saturated fat is low at approximately 1.8 g/100 g.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The grilled fish component supports anti-inflammatory dietary goals; request less teriyaki sauce to reduce sodium-driven tissue fluid retention, and substitute white rice with brown rice to lower the refined carbohydrate load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fish and Teriyaki Chicken Bento is a Japanese-style composite meal served in a bento box format, consisting of steamed white rice, a grilled fish fillet (typically salmon, mackerel, or white fish), and teriyaki-glazed chicken pieces, accompanied by assorted vegetables such as broccoli, carrots, or edamame. The dish is widely sold in Singapore hawker centres, food courts, and Japanese fast-casual restaurants. Per 100 g, it provides 198 kcal, 8.78 g protein, 7.33 g fat, and 24.2 g carbohydrate, with 217 mg sodium and 1.01 g dietary fibre. The teriyaki glaze — a mixture of soy sauce, mirin, sake, and sugar — imparts a sweet-savoury umami flavour and contributes the majority of the sodium content. The fish component provides beneficial omega-3 fatty acids, supporting anti-inflammatory and cardiovascular health goals. Clinically, the dish is high in glycaemic load due to white rice (GI ~72), making it a concern for Type 2 Diabetes management; sodium is borderline elevated at 217 mg/100 g, warranting caution for hypertension. The dish is halal-certified in most Singapore outlets and is dairy-free. A standard bento portion weighs approximately 400 g, delivering roughly 792 kcal, 35 g protein, and 97 g carbohydrate. Substituting brown rice and reducing teriyaki sauce significantly improves the nutritional profile for metabolic health.

How many calories in a bento box?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05100052) at confidence 0.95. GI is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate component (white rice, GI ~72) using the University of Sydney GI Database, which is the standard analogous-estimate approach for composite rice-based dishes. Saturated fat estimated at 1.8 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central data for comparable grilled fish and teriyaki chicken components.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available in HPB source): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. Detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this composite bento dish are not reported in the HPB Foodo database (F05100052) or confirmed in USDA FoodData Central for this specific preparation as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat of 1.8 g/100 g is estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for grilled fish and teriyaki chicken components (adapted estimate). GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on white rice GI from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this composite dish was found. Glycemic load per serving (400 g) calculated as (72 × 92.76 g net carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 66.8. The input record listed gi_value as 0 with confidence 0.339 from an 'Embedded GI Database' — this was rejected as a forbidden/unsupported source and replaced with the University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate. No core macro fields are zero; all macros are non-zero and sourced from HPB.
